key_themes: Recommended 71.5p cash takeover by Frankel UK Bidco (Long Path Partners-backed), with UK/French regulatory clearances now secured and May 2026 deadline
Solid FY25 results: +3% revenue to £89.8m, +10% recurring revenue to £59.7m (66% of total), stable 30% adj. EBITDA margin, record £108m order intake
Strong public-sector software visibility (planning, elections, assets, communities) with AI/geospatial focus and margin expansion targets
M&A integration (Plianz) driving net debt rise and lower free cash flow; no FY25 dividend due to offer
Low trading volume on X and forums; investor attention centered on acceptance levels and deal completion
summary: Idox plc reported steady FY25 results with resilient recurring revenue growth, stable high margins, and a record order book that gives excellent FY26 visibility in its regulated public-sector markets. Sentiment is firmly positive due to the board-recommended all-cash takeover at 71.5p by a Long Path Partners vehicle, which has now cleared key UK national-security and French FDI hurdles after an earlier pause. Shareholders are being urged to accept ahead of the mid-May deadline, with the stock trading close to the offer price; underlying operational progress supports the deal while limited organic growth and cash-flow pressures are secondary concerns in the current offer-driven environment.
sources: Web/news: 15+ results (Yahoo Finance, Investegate, LSE RNS, Armchair Trader, company site) covering FY25 results and frequent Form 8.3 disclosures, X/Twitter: ~10-15 relevant recent posts (semantic + keyword search); low overall volume, mostly news relays, Investor forums: ADVFN, LSE Share Chat, Stockopedia discussions (primarily bid-focused)
